g.text: Min-Heap Timer Data Structure — Memory Layout and Operations
Heap Array in Memory — Heap Property Satisfied
INSERT | O(log n) | ~13 comparisons | append + sift-up
EXTRACT-MIN | O(log n) | ~13 comparisons | swap root + sift-down
CANCEL | O(1) | 1 write | lazy delete, no restructure
PEEK-MIN | O(1) | 1 read nodes[0] | always root
BUILD-HEAP | O(n) | n/2 sift-downs | Floyd's algorithm
COMPACT | O(n log n) | rare (>50% cancel) | rebuild from scratch
Memory | O(n) | 10K*16B = 160KB | fits in L2/L3 cache
vs Sort.List| O(n) insert | 10K compares | 769x slower at insert
vs T.Wheel | O(1) both | 0 compares | more complex, fixed res
Heap array size | 10K*16B = 160KB | L2 (256KB) boundary — hot under load
Sift-up path | 13 nodes * 16B | 208B — 4 cache lines — L1 hot
Sift-down path | 13 nodes * 16B | 208B — 4 cache lines — L1 hot
Root access | nodes[0] | always L1 — most frequent access
CLOCK_MONOTONIC | vDSO call ~20ns | no syscall — kernel maps read-only page
Each epoll tick | 1 peek + ~50 cancel+insert | ~100 comparisons total
Step 1 | Append to nodes[size] | O(1) | size++
Step 2 | i = size - 1 | O(1) | start idx
Step 3 | SIFT-UP loop | O(log n) | restore heap
3d | i = parent; goto 3a | O(1) | move up
n=10K | max swaps = log2(10000) ~ 13 swaps | 13 iters
Step 1 | Skip cancelled root: pop+retry | O(log n)
Step 2 | result = nodes[0] | O(1) | save min
Step 3 | nodes[0] = nodes[size-1] | O(1) | move last
Step 5 | SIFT-DOWN from i=0 | O(log n) | restore
Step 1 | Find node by conn_id | O(n) naive / O(1) with index
Step 2 | node.cancelled = true | O(1) | mark, do NOT remove
Step 3 | heap.n_cancelled++ | O(1) | track for compaction
---- | No heap restructure needed | O(1) total
SKIP | extract_min skips cancelled nodes: pop+retry
COMPACT| When n_cancelled > size/2: rebuild | O(n log n)
Lazy delete avoids O(log n) remove; correct because
cancelled nodes are skipped during extract_min loop
